mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 8.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing market index above 700,000 units signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 700,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index below 94.5 signals a weakening industrial sector that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 94.5 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real GDP growth rate above 3.5% signals a strengthening economy that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 3.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in the payroll employment rate below 130,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 130,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real personal consumption expenditures above 226.5 signals a strengthening consumer sector that boosts corporate profitability and investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 226.5 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
